stemHtml: During cruise flight, the autopilot is being used to maintain the selected altitude. The pilot receives their descent clearance and selects idle thrust, which is normal for a descent, but forgets to select any descent mode in the autopilot system. As the autopilot continues to maintain the selected altitude…

options.a: the aeroplane will lose speed, resulting in a progressively higher nose attitude and rapidly increasing drag unless the pilot immediately takes action to prevent excessive speed loss.
options.b: the wings will have progressively increasing values of CL and induced drag, but the total drag remains constant due to reducing parasite drag; thus the speed remains constant.
options.c: the controls become more responsive, and the pilot must, if performing a manual recovery, use the controls with care to avoid overstressing the airframe.
options.d: the speed will stabilise at VMD since, at this point, the optimum relationship exists between the induced and parasite drag, and the aeroplane is speed stable at VMD.
